channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-20105] directory entry persists after mv
item.description:
We have observed an in-use directory entry persists on a node after being moved by another node. The original directory entry persists even after it is no longer in use, including logging out and back into the node. 

 

Example on two nodes, mutt6,mutt7

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ pwd
/p/lflood/defazio1/test_mv_dir
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ ls
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$

(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ pwd
/p/lflood/defazio1/test_mv_dir
(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ ls
(mutt7):test_mv_dir$

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mkdir -p p0/c0
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ touch p0/c0/
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ lfs getdirstripe p0
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: none
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ lfs getdirstripe p0/c0
lmv_stripe_count: 0 lmv_stripe_offset: 0 lmv_hash_type: none
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ tree
.
└── p0
    └── c0
        └── f2 directories, 1 file

(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ tree
.
└── p0
    └── c0
        └── f2 directories, 1 file
(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ cd p0/c0
(mutt7):c0$ ls
f

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mv p0/c0 p0/c1
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ tree
.
└── p0
    └── c1
        └── f2 directories, 1 file
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ ls p0/c0
ls: cannot access 'p0/c0': No such file or directory

(mutt7):c0$ ls
f
(mutt7):c0$ ls ..
c1
(mutt7):c0$ cd ..
(mutt7):p0$ ls
c1
(mutt7):p0$ ls c0
f
(mutt7):p0$ stat -c "%i" c0 c1
144127150650301722
144127150650301722
(mutt7):p0$ lfs path2fid  c0 c1
c0: [0x2000ae141:0x1d1a:0x0]
c1: [0x2000ae141:0x1d1a:0x0]
(mutt7):p0$ sudo lctl set_param ldlm.namespaces.lflood-MDT0000-mdc-*.lru_size=clear
ldlm.namespaces.lflood-MDT0000-mdc-ff1715b8d1174800.lru_size=clear
(mutt7):p0$ stat -c "%i" c0 c1
stat: cannot statx 'c0': No such file or directory
144127150650301722

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: I've added test-mv-dir-2026-04-15.tar.gz which has debug logs for the 2 clients nodes and the MDT.
Giancarlo, I couldn't reproduce on my local system with both 2.15 and master code, could you help collect debug log? Better to enable 'trace info inode dentry" for debug mask.

Yes, clearing the namespace works. I can also "rm -r" the directory.

 

(mutt7):p0$ ls
c1/
(mutt7):p0$ stat c0
  File: c0
  Size: 26624       Blocks: 52         IO Block: 131072 directory
Device: 11602e98h/291516056d    Inode: 144127150650301763  Links: 2
Access: (0700/drwx------)  Uid: (28153/defazio1)   Gid: (28153/defazio1)
Access: 2026-04-09 16:46:15.000000000 -0700
Modify: 2026-04-09 16:46:15.000000000 -0700
Change: 2026-04-09 16:46:23.000000000 -0700
 Birth: 2026-04-09 16:46:15.000000000 -0700
(mutt7):p0(1)$ rm -r c0
rm: cannot remove 'c0': No such file or directory
(mutt7):p0(1)$ stat c0
stat: cannot statx 'c0': No such file or directory
(mutt7):p0(1)$ ls
c1/

 

This happens consistently. However, I need to do some activity in the directory after the move for it to work. For example it doesn't happen with

 

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mkdir -p p0/c0

(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ cd p0/c0

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mv p0/c0 p0/c1

(mutt7):c0$ cd ..
(mutt7):p0$ ls
c1/
(mutt7):p0$ stat c0
stat: cannot statx 'c0': No such file or directory

 

 

but if I stat c0 before "cd .." then I can still stat it after "cd .."

 

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mkdir -p p0/c0

(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ cd p0/c0

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mv p0/c0 p0/c1

(mutt7):c0$ stat .
  File: .
  Size: 26624       Blocks: 52         IO Block: 131072 directory
Device: 11602e98h/291516056d    Inode: 144127150650301761  Links: 2
Access: (0700/drwx------)  Uid: (28153/defazio1)   Gid: (28153/defazio1)
Access: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700
Modify: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700
Change: 2026-04-09 16:40:35.000000000 -0700
 Birth: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700
(mutt7):c0$ cd ..
(mutt7):p0$ ls
c1/
(mutt7):p0$ stat c0
  File: c0
  Size: 26624       Blocks: 52         IO Block: 131072 directory
Device: 11602e98h/291516056d    Inode: 144127150650301761  Links: 2
Access: (0700/drwx------)  Uid: (28153/defazio1)   Gid: (28153/defazio1)
Access: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700
Modify: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700
Change: 2026-04-09 16:40:35.000000000 -0700
 Birth: 2026-04-09 16:40:10.000000000 -0700

 

It seems to only happen when the parent and child directories are on the same MDT. In the description example p0 and c0 are both on MDT 0.

If I put them on separate MDTs c0 goes away after "cd .."

 

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ lfs setdirstripe -i 2 p0
(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ lfs setdirstripe -i 3 p0/c0

(mutt7):test_mv_dir$ cd p0/c0

(mutt6):test_mv_dir$ mv p0/c0 p0/c1

(mutt7):c0$ stat .
  File: .
  Size: 26624       Blocks: 52         IO Block: 131072 directory
Device: 11602e98h/291516056d    Inode: 198171307093786625  Links: 2
Access: (0700/drwx------)  Uid: (28153/defazio1)   Gid: (28153/defazio1)
Access: 2026-04-09 16:51:57.000000000 -0700
Modify: 2026-04-09 16:51:57.000000000 -0700
Change: 2026-04-09 16:51:57.000000000 -0700
 Birth: 2026-04-09 16:51:57.000000000 -0700
(mutt7):c0$ cd ..
(mutt7):p0$ ls
c1/
(mutt7):p0$ stat c0
stat: cannot statx 'c0': No such file or directory

Hi Giancarlo,

Can you give us more information here - is this just once, or does this happen every time?

And it looks like it goes away after clearing the namespace?
